arguments.name: blazor-preview
arguments.prompt:
The user wants to preview exactly what files would be generated by creating a Blazor Web App project called "MyBlazorApp" in ./test-output with interactive server rendering — WITHOUT actually creating the project.

Use `dotnet new blazor` (the Blazor Web App template) with `--dry-run` to show the file list. The relevant parameters are:
- Project name: MyBlazorApp
- Output directory: ./test-output
- Interactive render mode: Server (use `--interactivity Server`)

Run this command:
```
dotnet new blazor --name MyBlazorApp --output ./test-output --interactivity Server --dry-run
```

Show the full output so the user can see every file that would be created.

data.content:
Here's the complete dry-run output. **No files were written to disk** — this is purely a preview. Here's a breakdown of what the Blazor Web App template with Server interactivity would generate:

### Project Root (`test-output/`)
| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `MyBlazorApp.csproj` | Project file |
| `Program.cs` | App entry point & service configuration |
| `appsettings.json` | Configuration |
| `appsettings.Development.json` | Dev-specific configuration |

### Components (`Components/`)
| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `App.razor` | Root application component |
| `Routes.razor` | Router configuration |
| `_Imports.razor` | Global `@using` directives |
| **Layout/** | |
| `MainLayout.razor` + `.css` | Main page layout with CSS isolation |
| `NavMenu.razor` + `.css` + `.js` | Side navigation menu |
| `ReconnectModal.razor` + `.css` + `.js` | SignalR reconnection UI (Server interactivity) |
| **Pages/** | |
| `Home.razor` | Home page |
| `Counter.razor` | Interactive counter demo |
| `Weather.razor` | Weather data page |
| `Error.razor` | Error page |
| `NotFound.razor` | 404 page |

### Static Assets (`wwwroot/`)
| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `app.css` | Application stylesheet |
| `favicon.png` | Browser tab icon |
| `lib/bootstrap/dist/css/*` | Bootstrap CSS (16 files — full, minified, RTL, source maps) |
| `lib/bootstrap/dist/js/*` | Bootstrap JS (12 files — bundle, ESM, standard, minified, source maps) |

### Properties
| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `launchSettings.json` | Local dev server profiles (HTTP/HTTPS ports, etc.) |

### Post-Creation Action
The template would also automatically run `dotnet restore` to pull in NuGet packages.

**Total: 68 files.** Note the `ReconnectModal` component — that's specific to the **Server** interactive render mode, providing UI for when the SignalR connection to the server drops.

When you're ready to actually create the project, just run the same command without `--dry-run`.

toolArgs.name: blazor-preview
toolArgs.prompt:
The user wants to preview exactly what files would be generated by creating a Blazor Web App project called "MyBlazorApp" in ./test-output with interactive server rendering — WITHOUT actually creating the project.

Use `dotnet new blazor` (the Blazor Web App template) with `--dry-run` to show the file list. The relevant parameters are:
- Project name: MyBlazorApp
- Output directory: ./test-output
- Interactive render mode: Server (use `--interactivity Server`)

Run this command:
```
dotnet new blazor --name MyBlazorApp --output ./test-output --interactivity Server --dry-run
```

Show the full output so the user can see every file that would be created.

data.content:
Here's what `dotnet new blazor --name MyBlazorApp --output ./test-output --interactivity Server` would generate (**68 files**, nothing created yet):

**Root** — `MyBlazorApp.csproj`, `Program.cs`, `appsettings.json`, `appsettings.Development.json`

**Components/**
- `App.razor`, `Routes.razor`, `_Imports.razor`
- **Layout/** — `MainLayout.razor` + `.css`, `NavMenu.razor` + `.css` + `.js`, `ReconnectModal.razor` + `.css` + `.js` (Server-specific reconnect UI)
- **Pages/** — `Home.razor`, `Counter.razor`, `Weather.razor`, `Error.razor`, `NotFound.razor`

**wwwroot/** — `app.css`, `favicon.png`, Bootstrap CSS (16 files) + JS (12 files)

**Properties/** — `launchSettings.json`

The `ReconnectModal` component is specific to **Server** interactivity — it handles SignalR reconnection UI. A `dotnet restore` would also run automatically as a post-action.

Say the word and I'll create it for real.
